person.name: Philip de Burnham
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Blomefield:

Walter, who held it at the survey, under the Earl, seems to be the ancestor of the family of de Burnham; Philip de Burnham was lord, in the reign of King Stephen, and had 2 sons, William and Reginald; William had a son Philip, who was lord in the 30th of Henry II. and one of the same name in Richard I; but William de Burnham dying without issue in the reign of Henry III. this lordship came to Sir William de Calthorp, by the marriage of his sister and heir, Cecilia, as may be seen in Harpley.

Some make the Burnhams to descend from a cousin-german of Hamelin Plantaginet Earl Warren and Surry, but as that Earl lived in the reign of Henry II and Richard I and as the Burnhams were lords long before their reigns. It is more probable that Walter was their ancestor.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burnham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Burnham. Those in Buckinghamshire (Burnham Beeches), Norfolk (various villages), and Essex (Burnham-on-Crouch) are named with Old English burna ‘stream’ + hām ‘homestead’. In the case of Burnham-on-Sea in Somerset, however, the second element is Old English hamm ‘water meadow’, while Burnham in Lincolnshire is named from brunnum, dative plural of Old Norse brunnr ‘spring’, originally used after a preposition, i.e. ‘(at) the springs’.

History: In 1635 Robert Burnham and his two brothers came from England to Ipswich, MA, after their ship was wrecked on the coast of Maine. In the mid 18th century John Burnham and his son, also called John, were among the early settlers in what became the state of VT. In 1785, the younger John Burnham established himself at Middletown, CT.

variants.name: Burnum
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Burnham  .

variants.name: Burnam
meanings: English: variant of   Burnham  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham was born in 1198, in Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Emma Strange. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham est né en 1198, à Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Emma Strange. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham wurde 1198 in Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England geboren. Er hatte mit Emma Strange mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham è nato il nel 1198 nel Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Emma Strange. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ko.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham(은)는 1198년 영국 노르폴크 번함 소에서 태어났다. 그는 Emma Strange(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham nasceu em 1198, em Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Emma Strange. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham родился в 1198 г. в местности бурнам таурп, норфолк, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Emma Strange. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Philip de Burnham nació en 1198, en Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Emma Strange. Murió en su pueblo.
